The build exited with code 1 due to Prettier formatting issues. While all tests passed successfully (6020 tests for erk, 136 for erk-dev, 115 for erk-statusline, and 26 for erk-mcp), the CI check failed because 6 files have formatting style issues:

session.assistant: The only failures are pre-existing Prettier formatting issues in unrelated files (docs, workflows, skill assets) — none of them are files I modified. All tests pass, ruff and ty pass. CI is green for everything that matters to this plan.

session.tool_result: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable
session.assistant:
Plan execution complete. Here's a summary of what was done:
**PR #9214 submitted**: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9214
### Changes made:
**`operation.py`** (245 → 709 lines) — received all business logic: - `ValidatedPlannedPR` dataclass - `LocalPrDispatchRequest` dataclass - `load_workflow_config()` — pure function, moved from cli.py - `_build_workflow_run_url()` / `_build_pr_url()` — pure helpers, moved from cli.py - `detect_pr_number_from_context()` — renamed (dropped leading `_`), moved from cli.py - `validate_planned_pr()` — replaced `_validate_planned_pr_for_dispatch()`, returns `MachineCommandError` instead of `SystemExit` - `dispatch_planned_pr()` — extracted from `_dispatch_planned_pr_plan()`, returns `MachineCommandError` on push failure / plan not found - `run_local_pr_dispatch()` — new orchestrator analogous to `run_pr_dispatch()`, handles auto-detection, base branch resolution, validate-then-dispatch loop
**`cli.py`** (662 → 158 lines) — stripped to thin adapter: - Kept `_print_dispatch_summary()` (pure display) - `pr_dispatch()` Click command simplified to determine mode, call operation functions, handle errors, display summary - Re-exports `load_workflow_config` for backwards compatibility
**Import updates** in 3 files: `incremental_dispatch.py`, `test_workflow_config.py`, `test_dispatch_cmd.py` all point to `operation.py` now.
